name: Sigma Concept Explainer
description: Design tokens for single-page concept explainers and interactive examples. Bauhaus-on-dark editorial: a warm near-black canvas, refined off-white ink, primary-colour accents used sparingly, a high-contrast display serif over a geometric grotesque, and monospace for technical labels.

accent.note: Accents are sparks, not fields. One accent leads (red); blue and yellow support inside diagrams only.

atmosphere.type: fixed dual radial gradients on the canvas

eyebrow.note: Pair with a 34x2px accent bar before the text, gap 0.8rem.

heroAccent.note: One emphasised word in the hero, set italic in faint ink.

lede.note: Keep to two lines for grid alignment.

motion.reducedMotion: Honour prefers-reduced-motion: disable all animation and transition, reveal content statically.

stage.purpose: the demo canvas inside a card
stage.technique: Stack absolutely-positioned layers; cross-fade with opacity (layerFade) to switch between neutral and principle-on states.

stateTag.note: Mirrors the toggle: names the current state of the demo.

eyebrow.decoration: 34x2px solid bar in accent.primary preceding the text

principles: Dark canvas, light ink: the page is warm near-black; off-white carries the content.
Accents are punctuation, not paint. Lead with one accent; reserve blue/yellow for inside diagrams.
Three voices of type: a display serif for the hero, a geometric grotesque for body, monospace for any technical or state label.
Cards are common regions. Equal-weight cards in a two-up grid; let enclosure and proximity do the grouping.
One focal point per view. The eyebrow's accent bar or a single coloured element is the eye's first landing.
Interaction over illustration. Each demo toggles between a neutral and an active state so the idea is felt, not just shown.
Restraint in motion. One orchestrated staggered entrance; quiet cross-fades on interaction; always respect reduced-motion.
Generous negative space and a contained measure (~64ch for intro prose, two-line ledes) keep hierarchy calm and legible.
